Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|12=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|12=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|12=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|12=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|12=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category: pages]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Onsen}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsukioka Onsen |image = 160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan02s3.jpg |Comment = Tsukioka Onsen{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|marker=}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|12=draft}} Tsukioka Onsen |Latitude Degrees = 37 |Latitude Minutes = 52 |Latitude Seconds = 49 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 18 |Longitude Seconds = 33 | E/W = E |Access = See the "Access" section |Water Type = {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing Sodium Chloride Hot Spring|lt_ja=含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|12=draft}} |Temperature = 50 |Discharge = |pH = 8 |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }} '''Tsukioka Onsen''' (月岡温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|12=draft}}. It is renowned for its high sulfur content—the very address of the hot spring is designated "Tsukioka Onsen." == Overview == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan21s3.jpg|thumb|300px|A street lined with hot spring inns (photographed in July 2016)]] Tsukioka Onsen is one of the premier hot spring resorts of Niigata Prefecture and is annually ranked among "Japan's 100 Hot Springs" (in 2020, it ranked 16th overall and 8th for water quality{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430020121900701.pdf|title=第34回「にっぽんの温泉100選」全ランキング|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2020-12-19|page=7}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430021010101501.pdf|title=第34回にっぽんの温泉100選 選んだ理由別順位|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2021-01-01|page=15}}). The onsen town comprises a district of inns and hotels (the Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association website lists 12 such establishments{{Cite web|url=http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hotel/|title=月岡温泉内の旅館ホテル一覧|publisher=Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association|accessdate=2021-05-30}}) along with a shopping street. A variety of experiences are available here—from grand traditional inns frequently chosen in "Japan’s 100 Best Hotels and Inns" by leading travel newspaper sponsors, to establishments awarded by online reservation sites like {{ill|Rakuten Travel|ja|楽天トラベル|12=draft}}, to venues offering theatrical and professional wrestling events, and even inns where visitors can enjoy traditional therapeutic bathing (湯治). Since its opening in Taishō 4 (1915), and especially from around 2014 when events were staged to mark its 100th anniversary, a host of revitalization initiatives have been implemented, ranging from efforts led by the tourism association to those undertaken by individual inns and shops.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title=平成28th year 度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=Niigata Prefecture Tourism Bureau, Tourism Planning Division|accessdate=2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=Niigata Keizai Shimbun|date=2018-09-10}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|The onsen town (photographed in July 2016)]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|"Tsukiakari no Niwa" (Garden of Moonlight)]] Located on the eastern edge of the Echigo Plain, Tsukioka Onsen once shone as "Niigata’s inner sanctuary." However, during the bubble economy its annual visitor count reached 1.2 million, and following the bubble collapse, visitor numbers were halved and two-thirds of its inns closed down{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100th year 空き家で米・酒|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei MJ|ja|日経MJ|12=draft}}|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=Machikou Net|accessdate=2020-07-05}}. Furthermore, trends toward larger inns and in-house customer retention diminished the traditional role of the onsen town, once leading some to claim that "there is nothing here but the hot spring.". In response, efforts to revitalize Tsukioka Onsen into an onsen town that invites leisurely strolls have been implemented. In conjunction with its 100th anniversary, initiatives for landscape improvement and store renovation have been vigorously pursued. In 2014, the tourism association redeveloped the once-neglected birthplace of the hot spring into "Gensen no Mori," a park where visitors can sample the hot spring water. Additionally, the shopping street's exterior was revamped with a brown-toned aesthetic. In 2018, a traditional Japanese garden, "Tsukiakari no Niwa," was established on a vacant lot formerly occupied by an inn. Moreover, a company named Miraizu, founded by young inn managers, has repurposed vacant houses to open a series of experiential stores that complement rather than compete with existing shops. The first such venture, "Niigata Jizake Kura" (a sake warehouse), opened in 2014, followed by "Niigata Jimono Umami" (dealing in local dried fish and fermented foods, 2015), "Niigata Rice Snack Den" (offering hand-roasted senbei, 2016), "Niigata Inbutsu Kaori" (selling wine and coffee stored in snow cellars, 2017), "Niigata Rice Flour Bei" (a specialty shop for rice flour products, 2018), and "Niigata Saika Minori" (a smoothie and gelato specialty store, 2019). In November 2020, a craft beer brewery also opened{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|date=2020-11-24}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sodium Chloride Hot Spring with Sulfur|ja|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|12=draft}}) (weakly alkaline, low tonicity, high-temperature hot spring)[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ About Tsukioka Onsen] - Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association. Accessed December 23, 2018. * The source temperature is approximately 50℃. * The hot spring water in the bathtubs exhibits a striking emerald green transparency and is reputed to have beautifying effects. It is counted among the "Iban-e Three Beautiful Hot Springs" along with {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|12=draft}}. Its sulfur content is notably high—second only to {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=draft}} in Japan. Unusually for a sulfur spring, it is weakly alkaline and gentle on the skin. Moreover, its chloride properties confer a high thermal effect, making the hot spring less prone to cooling and thus highly beneficial for health.
Bathing in this sulfur spring is believed to have an effective beautifying impact on the skin, potentially leading to a lighter complexion, and leaving the skin exceptionally smooth—hence its reputation as the "hot spring that can make you beautiful." Although its water is a vivid emerald green that might be mistaken for a bath additive, its color may shift to milky white under certain seasonal or weather conditions. It is said that in the past, people would predict the next day’s weather by observing the hue of the hot spring water. == Surroundings == Adjacent to the onsen town is the {{ill|Tsukioka Kari-on Park|ja|月岡カリオンパーク|12=draft}}, which houses a glassmaking experience facility and a sword heritage hall. Additionally, a public bath, "Moto-yu Bijin no Izumi" (Origin of the Beautiful Spring), is available. == History == A stone monument marking the "Birthplace of Tsukioka Onsen" stands near a drilling well from which water over 50℃ flows, and it is said that Shūzō Honma built a public bath there, marking the inception of Tsukioka Onsen. Adjacent to this public bath, "Aoki-kan" opened as a therapeutic lodging; subsequently, numerous therapeutic inns such as "Naniwaya" emerged, along with self-catering shops like "Shinkikuyā" (collectively referred to as the "Old Yu"). In the late Taishō to early Shōwa periods, a new hot spring district was developed near the current footbath area, centered on a two-story public bath (referred to as the "New Yu"). Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|12=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|12=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|12=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|12=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|12=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|12=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category: pages]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Onsen}}

The first such venture, "Niigata Jizake Kura" (a sake warehouse), opened in 2014, followed by "Niigata Jimono Umami" (dealing in local dried fish and fermented foods, 2015), "Niigata Rice Snack Den" (offering hand-roasted senbei, 2016), "Niigata Inbutsu Kaori" (selling wine and coffee stored in snow cellars, 2017), "Niigata Rice Flour Bei" (a specialty shop for rice flour products, 2018), and "Niigata Saika Minori" (a smoothie and gelato specialty store, 2019). In November 2020, a craft beer brewery also opened{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|date=2020-11-24}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sodium Chloride Hot Spring with Sulfur|ja|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|12=draft}}) (weakly alkaline, low tonicity, high-temperature hot spring)[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ About Tsukioka Onsen] - Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association. Accessed December 23, 2018. * The source temperature is approximately 50℃. * The hot spring water in the bathtubs exhibits a striking emerald green transparency and is reputed to have beautifying effects. It is counted among the "Iban-e Three Beautiful Hot Springs" along with {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|12=draft}}. Its sulfur content is notably high—second only to {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=draft}} in Japan. Unusually for a sulfur spring, it is weakly alkaline and gentle on the skin. Moreover, its chloride properties confer a high thermal effect, making the hot spring less prone to cooling and thus highly beneficial for health.
Bathing in this sulfur spring is believed to have an effective beautifying impact on the skin, potentially leading to a lighter complexion, and leaving the skin exceptionally smooth—hence its reputation as the "hot spring that can make you beautiful." Although its water is a vivid emerald green that might be mistaken for a bath additive, its color may shift to milky white under certain seasonal or weather conditions. It is said that in the past, people would predict the next day’s weather by observing the hue of the hot spring water. == Surroundings == Adjacent to the onsen town is the {{ill|Tsukioka Kari-on Park|ja|月岡カリオンパーク|12=draft}}, which houses a glassmaking experience facility and a sword heritage hall. Additionally, a public bath, "Moto-yu Bijin no Izumi" (Origin of the Beautiful Spring), is available. == History == A stone monument marking the "Birthplace of Tsukioka Onsen" stands near a drilling well from which water over 50℃ flows, and it is said that Shūzō Honma built a public bath there, marking the inception of Tsukioka Onsen. Adjacent to this public bath, "Aoki-kan" opened as a therapeutic lodging; subsequently, numerous therapeutic inns such as "Naniwaya" emerged, along with self-catering shops like "Shinkikuyā" (collectively referred to as the "Old Yu"). In the late Taishō to early Shōwa periods, a new hot spring district was developed near the current footbath area, centered on a two-story public bath (referred to as the "New Yu"). Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|12=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|12=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|12=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|12=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|12=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|12=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category: pages]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Onsen}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsukioka Onsen |image = 160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan02s3.jpg |Comment = Tsukioka Onsen{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|marker=}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|12=draft}} Tsukioka Onsen |Latitude Degrees = 37 |Latitude Minutes = 52 |Latitude Seconds = 49 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 18 |Longitude Seconds = 33 | E/W = E |Access = See the "Access" section |Water Type = {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing Sodium Chloride Hot Spring|lt_ja=含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|12=draft}} |Temperature = 50 |Discharge = |pH = 8 |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }} '''Tsukioka Onsen''' (月岡温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|12=draft}}. It is renowned for its high sulfur content—the very address of the hot spring is designated "Tsukioka Onsen." == Overview == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan21s3.jpg|thumb|300px|A street lined with hot spring inns (photographed in July 2016)]] Tsukioka Onsen is one of the premier hot spring resorts of Niigata Prefecture and is annually ranked among "Japan's 100 Hot Springs" (in 2020, it ranked 16th overall and 8th for water quality{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430020121900701.pdf|title=第34回「にっぽんの温泉100選」全ランキング|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2020-12-19|page=7}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430021010101501.pdf|title=第34回にっぽんの温泉100選 選んだ理由別順位|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2021-01-01|page=15}}). The onsen town comprises a district of inns and hotels (the Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association website lists 12 such establishments{{Cite web|url=http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hotel/|title=月岡温泉内の旅館ホテル一覧|publisher=Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association|accessdate=2021-05-30}}) along with a shopping street. A variety of experiences are available here—from grand traditional inns frequently chosen in "Japan’s 100 Best Hotels and Inns" by leading travel newspaper sponsors, to establishments awarded by online reservation sites like {{ill|Rakuten Travel|ja|楽天トラベル|12=draft}}, to venues offering theatrical and professional wrestling events, and even inns where visitors can enjoy traditional therapeutic bathing (湯治). Since its opening in Taishō 4 (1915), and especially from around 2014 when events were staged to mark its 100th anniversary, a host of revitalization initiatives have been implemented, ranging from efforts led by the tourism association to those undertaken by individual inns and shops.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title=平成28th year 度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=Niigata Prefecture Tourism Bureau, Tourism Planning Division|accessdate=2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=Niigata Keizai Shimbun|date=2018-09-10}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|The onsen town (photographed in July 2016)]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|"Tsukiakari no Niwa" (Garden of Moonlight)]] Located on the eastern edge of the Echigo Plain, Tsukioka Onsen once shone as "Niigata’s inner sanctuary." However, during the bubble economy its annual visitor count reached 1.2 million, and following the bubble collapse, visitor numbers were halved and two-thirds of its inns closed down{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100th year 空き家で米・酒|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei MJ|ja|日経MJ|12=draft}}|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=Machikou Net|accessdate=2020-07-05}}. Furthermore, trends toward larger inns and in-house customer retention diminished the traditional role of the onsen town, once leading some to claim that "there is nothing here but the hot spring.". In response, efforts to revitalize Tsukioka Onsen into an onsen town that invites leisurely strolls have been implemented. In conjunction with its 100th anniversary, initiatives for landscape improvement and store renovation have been vigorously pursued. In 2014, the tourism association redeveloped the once-neglected birthplace of the hot spring into "Gensen no Mori," a park where visitors can sample the hot spring water. Additionally, the shopping street's exterior was revamped with a brown-toned aesthetic. In 2018, a traditional Japanese garden, "Tsukiakari no Niwa," was established on a vacant lot formerly occupied by an inn. Moreover, a company named Miraizu, founded by young inn managers, has repurposed vacant houses to open a series of experiential stores that complement rather than compete with existing shops. The first such venture, "Niigata Jizake Kura" (a sake warehouse), opened in 2014, followed by "Niigata Jimono Umami" (dealing in local dried fish and fermented foods, 2015), "Niigata Rice Snack Den" (offering hand-roasted senbei, 2016), "Niigata Inbutsu Kaori" (selling wine and coffee stored in snow cellars, 2017), "Niigata Rice Flour Bei" (a specialty shop for rice flour products, 2018), and "Niigata Saika Minori" (a smoothie and gelato specialty store, 2019). In November 2020, a craft beer brewery also opened{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|date=2020-11-24}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sodium Chloride Hot Spring with Sulfur|ja|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|12=draft}}) (weakly alkaline, low tonicity, high-temperature hot spring)[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ About Tsukioka Onsen] - Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association. Accessed December 23, 2018. * The source temperature is approximately 50℃. * The hot spring water in the bathtubs exhibits a striking emerald green transparency and is reputed to have beautifying effects. It is counted among the "Iban-e Three Beautiful Hot Springs" along with {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|12=draft}}. Its sulfur content is notably high—second only to {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=draft}} in Japan. Unusually for a sulfur spring, it is weakly alkaline and gentle on the skin. Moreover, its chloride properties confer a high thermal effect, making the hot spring less prone to cooling and thus highly beneficial for health.
Bathing in this sulfur spring is believed to have an effective beautifying impact on the skin, potentially leading to a lighter complexion, and leaving the skin exceptionally smooth—hence its reputation as the "hot spring that can make you beautiful." Although its water is a vivid emerald green that might be mistaken for a bath additive, its color may shift to milky white under certain seasonal or weather conditions. It is said that in the past, people would predict the next day’s weather by observing the hue of the hot spring water. == Surroundings == Adjacent to the onsen town is the {{ill|Tsukioka Kari-on Park|ja|月岡カリオンパーク|12=draft}}, which houses a glassmaking experience facility and a sword heritage hall. Additionally, a public bath, "Moto-yu Bijin no Izumi" (Origin of the Beautiful Spring), is available. == History == A stone monument marking the "Birthplace of Tsukioka Onsen" stands near a drilling well from which water over 50℃ flows, and it is said that Shūzō Honma built a public bath there, marking the inception of Tsukioka Onsen. Adjacent to this public bath, "Aoki-kan" opened as a therapeutic lodging; subsequently, numerous therapeutic inns such as "Naniwaya" emerged, along with self-catering shops like "Shinkikuyā" (collectively referred to as the "Old Yu"). In the late Taishō to early Shōwa periods, a new hot spring district was developed near the current footbath area, centered on a two-story public bath (referred to as the "New Yu"). Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|12=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|12=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|12=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|12=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|12=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|12=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category: pages]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Onsen}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsukioka Onsen |image = 160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan02s3.jpg |Comment = Tsukioka Onsen{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|marker=}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|12=draft}} Tsukioka Onsen |Latitude Degrees = 37 |Latitude Minutes = 52 |Latitude Seconds = 49 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 18 |Longitude Seconds = 33 | E/W = E |Access = See the "Access" section |Water Type = {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing Sodium Chloride Hot Spring|lt_ja=含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|12=draft}} |Temperature = 50 |Discharge = |pH = 8 |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }} '''Tsukioka Onsen''' (月岡温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|12=draft}}. It is renowned for its high sulfur content—the very address of the hot spring is designated "Tsukioka Onsen." == Overview == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan21s3.jpg|thumb|300px|A street lined with hot spring inns (photographed in July 2016)]] Tsukioka Onsen is one of the premier hot spring resorts of Niigata Prefecture and is annually ranked among "Japan's 100 Hot Springs" (in 2020, it ranked 16th overall and 8th for water quality{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430020121900701.pdf|title=第34回「にっぽんの温泉100選」全ランキング|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2020-12-19|page=7}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430021010101501.pdf|title=第34回にっぽんの温泉100選 選んだ理由別順位|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2021-01-01|page=15}}). The onsen town comprises a district of inns and hotels (the Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association website lists 12 such establishments{{Cite web|url=http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hotel/|title=月岡温泉内の旅館ホテル一覧|publisher=Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association|accessdate=2021-05-30}}) along with a shopping street. A variety of experiences are available here—from grand traditional inns frequently chosen in "Japan’s 100 Best Hotels and Inns" by leading travel newspaper sponsors, to establishments awarded by online reservation sites like {{ill|Rakuten Travel|ja|楽天トラベル|12=draft}}, to venues offering theatrical and professional wrestling events, and even inns where visitors can enjoy traditional therapeutic bathing (湯治). Since its opening in Taishō 4 (1915), and especially from around 2014 when events were staged to mark its 100th anniversary, a host of revitalization initiatives have been implemented, ranging from efforts led by the tourism association to those undertaken by individual inns and shops.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title=平成28th year 度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=Niigata Prefecture Tourism Bureau, Tourism Planning Division|accessdate=2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=Niigata Keizai Shimbun|date=2018-09-10}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|The onsen town (photographed in July 2016)]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|"Tsukiakari no Niwa" (Garden of Moonlight)]] Located on the eastern edge of the Echigo Plain, Tsukioka Onsen once shone as "Niigata’s inner sanctuary." However, during the bubble economy its annual visitor count reached 1.2 million, and following the bubble collapse, visitor numbers were halved and two-thirds of its inns closed down{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100th year 空き家で米・酒|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei MJ|ja|日経MJ|12=draft}}|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=Machikou Net|accessdate=2020-07-05}}. Furthermore, trends toward larger inns and in-house customer retention diminished the traditional role of the onsen town, once leading some to claim that "there is nothing here but the hot spring.". In response, efforts to revitalize Tsukioka Onsen into an onsen town that invites leisurely strolls have been implemented. In conjunction with its 100th anniversary, initiatives for landscape improvement and store renovation have been vigorously pursued. In 2014, the tourism association redeveloped the once-neglected birthplace of the hot spring into "Gensen no Mori," a park where visitors can sample the hot spring water. Additionally, the shopping street's exterior was revamped with a brown-toned aesthetic. In 2018, a traditional Japanese garden, "Tsukiakari no Niwa," was established on a vacant lot formerly occupied by an inn. Moreover, a company named Miraizu, founded by young inn managers, has repurposed vacant houses to open a series of experiential stores that complement rather than compete with existing shops. The first such venture, "Niigata Jizake Kura" (a sake warehouse), opened in 2014, followed by "Niigata Jimono Umami" (dealing in local dried fish and fermented foods, 2015), "Niigata Rice Snack Den" (offering hand-roasted senbei, 2016), "Niigata Inbutsu Kaori" (selling wine and coffee stored in snow cellars, 2017), "Niigata Rice Flour Bei" (a specialty shop for rice flour products, 2018), and "Niigata Saika Minori" (a smoothie and gelato specialty store, 2019). In November 2020, a craft beer brewery also opened{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|date=2020-11-24}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sodium Chloride Hot Spring with Sulfur|ja|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|12=draft}}) (weakly alkaline, low tonicity, high-temperature hot spring)[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ About Tsukioka Onsen] - Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association. Accessed December 23, 2018. * The source temperature is approximately 50℃. * The hot spring water in the bathtubs exhibits a striking emerald green transparency and is reputed to have beautifying effects. It is counted among the "Iban-e Three Beautiful Hot Springs" along with {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|12=draft}}. Its sulfur content is notably high—second only to {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=draft}} in Japan. Unusually for a sulfur spring, it is weakly alkaline and gentle on the skin. Moreover, its chloride properties confer a high thermal effect, making the hot spring less prone to cooling and thus highly beneficial for health.
Bathing in this sulfur spring is believed to have an effective beautifying impact on the skin, potentially leading to a lighter complexion, and leaving the skin exceptionally smooth—hence its reputation as the "hot spring that can make you beautiful." Although its water is a vivid emerald green that might be mistaken for a bath additive, its color may shift to milky white under certain seasonal or weather conditions. It is said that in the past, people would predict the next day’s weather by observing the hue of the hot spring water. == Surroundings == Adjacent to the onsen town is the {{ill|Tsukioka Kari-on Park|ja|月岡カリオンパーク|12=draft}}, which houses a glassmaking experience facility and a sword heritage hall. Additionally, a public bath, "Moto-yu Bijin no Izumi" (Origin of the Beautiful Spring), is available. == History == A stone monument marking the "Birthplace of Tsukioka Onsen" stands near a drilling well from which water over 50℃ flows, and it is said that Shūzō Honma built a public bath there, marking the inception of Tsukioka Onsen. Adjacent to this public bath, "Aoki-kan" opened as a therapeutic lodging; subsequently, numerous therapeutic inns such as "Naniwaya" emerged, along with self-catering shops like "Shinkikuyā" (collectively referred to as the "Old Yu"). In the late Taishō to early Shōwa periods, a new hot spring district was developed near the current footbath area, centered on a two-story public bath (referred to as the "New Yu"). Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|12=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|12=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|12=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|12=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|12=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|12=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category: pages]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] [[Category:New]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Onsen}}

To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|12=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|12=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|12=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|12=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|12=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] [[Category:New]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Onsen}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsukioka Onsen |image = 160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan02s3.jpg |Comment = Tsukioka Onsen{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|marker=}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|12=draft}} Tsukioka Onsen |Latitude Degrees = 37 |Latitude Minutes = 52 |Latitude Seconds = 49 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 18 |Longitude Seconds = 33 | E/W = E |Access = See the "Access" section |Water Type = {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing Sodium Chloride Hot Spring|lt_ja=含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|12=draft}} |Temperature = 50 |Discharge = |pH = 8 |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }} '''Tsukioka Onsen''' (月岡温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|12=draft}}. It is renowned for its high sulfur content—the very address of the hot spring is designated "Tsukioka Onsen." == Overview == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan21s3.jpg|thumb|300px|A street lined with hot spring inns (photographed in July 2016)]] Tsukioka Onsen is one of the premier hot spring resorts of Niigata Prefecture and is annually ranked among "Japan's 100 Hot Springs" (in 2020, it ranked 16th overall and 8th for water quality{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430020121900701.pdf|title=第34回「にっぽんの温泉100選」全ランキング|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2020-12-19|page=7}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430021010101501.pdf|title=第34回にっぽんの温泉100選 選んだ理由別順位|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2021-01-01|page=15}}). The onsen town comprises a district of inns and hotels (the Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association website lists 12 such establishments{{Cite web|url=http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hotel/|title=月岡温泉内の旅館ホテル一覧|publisher=Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association|accessdate=2021-05-30}}) along with a shopping street. A variety of experiences are available here—from grand traditional inns frequently chosen in "Japan’s 100 Best Hotels and Inns" by leading travel newspaper sponsors, to establishments awarded by online reservation sites like {{ill|Rakuten Travel|ja|楽天トラベル|12=draft}}, to venues offering theatrical and professional wrestling events, and even inns where visitors can enjoy traditional therapeutic bathing (湯治). Since its opening in Taishō 4 (1915), and especially from around 2014 when events were staged to mark its 100th anniversary, a host of revitalization initiatives have been implemented, ranging from efforts led by the tourism association to those undertaken by individual inns and shops.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title=平成28th year 度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=Niigata Prefecture Tourism Bureau, Tourism Planning Division|accessdate=2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=Niigata Keizai Shimbun|date=2018-09-10}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|The onsen town (photographed in July 2016)]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|"Tsukiakari no Niwa" (Garden of Moonlight)]] Located on the eastern edge of the Echigo Plain, Tsukioka Onsen once shone as "Niigata’s inner sanctuary." However, during the bubble economy its annual visitor count reached 1.2 million, and following the bubble collapse, visitor numbers were halved and two-thirds of its inns closed down{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100th year 空き家で米・酒|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei MJ|ja|日経MJ|12=draft}}|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=Machikou Net|accessdate=2020-07-05}}. Furthermore, trends toward larger inns and in-house customer retention diminished the traditional role of the onsen town, once leading some to claim that "there is nothing here but the hot spring.". In response, efforts to revitalize Tsukioka Onsen into an onsen town that invites leisurely strolls have been implemented. In conjunction with its 100th anniversary, initiatives for landscape improvement and store renovation have been vigorously pursued. In 2014, the tourism association redeveloped the once-neglected birthplace of the hot spring into "Gensen no Mori," a park where visitors can sample the hot spring water. Additionally, the shopping street's exterior was revamped with a brown-toned aesthetic. In 2018, a traditional Japanese garden, "Tsukiakari no Niwa," was established on a vacant lot formerly occupied by an inn. Moreover, a company named Miraizu, founded by young inn managers, has repurposed vacant houses to open a series of experiential stores that complement rather than compete with existing shops. The first such venture, "Niigata Jizake Kura" (a sake warehouse), opened in 2014, followed by "Niigata Jimono Umami" (dealing in local dried fish and fermented foods, 2015), "Niigata Rice Snack Den" (offering hand-roasted senbei, 2016), "Niigata Inbutsu Kaori" (selling wine and coffee stored in snow cellars, 2017), "Niigata Rice Flour Bei" (a specialty shop for rice flour products, 2018), and "Niigata Saika Minori" (a smoothie and gelato specialty store, 2019). In November 2020, a craft beer brewery also opened{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|date=2020-11-24}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sodium Chloride Hot Spring with Sulfur|ja|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|12=draft}}) (weakly alkaline, low tonicity, high-temperature hot spring)[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ About Tsukioka Onsen] - Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association. Accessed December 23, 2018. * The source temperature is approximately 50℃. * The hot spring water in the bathtubs exhibits a striking emerald green transparency and is reputed to have beautifying effects. It is counted among the "Iban-e Three Beautiful Hot Springs" along with {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|12=draft}}. Its sulfur content is notably high—second only to {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=draft}} in Japan. Unusually for a sulfur spring, it is weakly alkaline and gentle on the skin. Moreover, its chloride properties confer a high thermal effect, making the hot spring less prone to cooling and thus highly beneficial for health.
Bathing in this sulfur spring is believed to have an effective beautifying impact on the skin, potentially leading to a lighter complexion, and leaving the skin exceptionally smooth—hence its reputation as the "hot spring that can make you beautiful." Although its water is a vivid emerald green that might be mistaken for a bath additive, its color may shift to milky white under certain seasonal or weather conditions. It is said that in the past, people would predict the next day’s weather by observing the hue of the hot spring water. == Surroundings == Adjacent to the onsen town is the {{ill|Tsukioka Kari-on Park|ja|月岡カリオンパーク|12=draft}}, which houses a glassmaking experience facility and a sword heritage hall. Additionally, a public bath, "Moto-yu Bijin no Izumi" (Origin of the Beautiful Spring), is available. == History == A stone monument marking the "Birthplace of Tsukioka Onsen" stands near a drilling well from which water over 50℃ flows, and it is said that Shūzō Honma built a public bath there, marking the inception of Tsukioka Onsen. Adjacent to this public bath, "Aoki-kan" opened as a therapeutic lodging; subsequently, numerous therapeutic inns such as "Naniwaya" emerged, along with self-catering shops like "Shinkikuyā" (collectively referred to as the "Old Yu"). In the late Taishō to early Shōwa periods, a new hot spring district was developed near the current footbath area, centered on a two-story public bath (referred to as the "New Yu"). Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|12=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|12=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|12=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|12=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|12=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|12=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|12=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|12=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|12=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] [[Category:New]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Onsen}}
