revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 月岡温泉 |画像 = 160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan02s3.jpg |コメント = 月岡温泉{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|marker=}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[新発田市]]月岡温泉 | 緯度度 = 37 | 緯度分 = 52 | 緯度秒 = 49 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 18 | 経度秒 = 33 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |交通アクセス = [[#アクセス|アクセス]]の項を参照 |泉質 = [[硫黄泉|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉]] |泉温 = 50 |湧出量 = |pH = 8 |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''月岡温泉'''（つきおかおんせん）は、[[新潟県]][[新発田市]]にある[[温泉]]。硫黄含有量の多さで知られる。温泉が所在する住所も「月岡温泉」となっている。 == 概要 == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan21s3.jpg|thumb|300px|温泉旅館が立ち並ぶ通り（2016年7月）]] 新潟県を代表する[[温泉地]]であり、[[にっぽんの温泉100選]]に例年ランクインする（2020年度は総合ランキングで全国16位{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430020121900701.pdf|title=第34回「にっぽんの温泉100選」全ランキング|publisher=[[観光経済新聞]]|date=2020-12-19|page=7}}、泉質で同8位{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430021010101501.pdf|title=第34回にっぽんの温泉100選 選んだ理由別順位|publisher=観光経済新聞|date=2021-01-01|page=15}}）。[[旅館]]・[[ホテル]]（月岡温泉観光協会ウェブサイト掲載施設は12軒{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hotel/|title=月岡温泉内の旅館ホテル一覧|publisher=月岡温泉観光協会|accessdate=2021-05-30}}）と[[商店街]]からなる[[温泉街]]がある。 [[旬刊旅行新聞|旅行新聞新社]]主催の「プロが選ぶ日本のホテル・旅館100選」で毎年上位に選出される日本を代表する大旅館から、宿泊予約サイト「[[楽天トラベル]]」からアワードやマイスターに選ばれた旅館、観劇やプロレスなどを開催する旅館、昔ながらの[[湯治]]を楽しめる旅館まで、様々な楽しみ方が出来る[[温泉地]]。[[大正]]4年（1915年）の開湯より100年を控えた2014年前後から各種の催しが開催され、近年は観光協会を中心としたものから、各旅館や商店が行うものまで「町おこし」が盛んになっている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title= 平成28年度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書 |publisher= 新潟県観光局 観光企画課|accessdate= 2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=にいがた経済新聞|date=2018-09-10}}。 == 温泉街 == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|温泉街（2016年7月）]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|「月あかりの庭」]] [[越後平野]]東縁に位置し、「新潟の奥座敷」として脚光を浴びていた温泉地だが、[[バブル経済]]期に年120万人いた観光客数は[[バブル崩壊]]後に半減し、旅館も3分の2が廃業・閉鎖された{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100年空き家で米・酒|newspaper=[[日経MJ]] 観光・インバウンド面|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=まち交ネット|accessdate=2020-07-05}}。また、旅館の大型化や旅館の自社施設内での顧客囲い込みが進んだため温泉街の役割が低下し、一時は「温泉以外は何もない」と言われた。 その後、「歩きたくなる温泉街」を目指して再生の取り組みが行われ、上記の開湯100年に合わせて、景観整備や店舗リノベーションの取り組みが続々と行われた。 2014年には観光協会が、荒れ地になっていた温泉発祥地を[[飲泉]]もできる「[[源泉]]の杜」として整備。さらに商店街の外観をなるべく茶色にする景観改善が行われた。2018年には、旅館跡の空き地に和風庭園「月あかりの庭」が設けられた{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228038.pdf|title=平成30年度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=新潟県観光局 観光企画課|accessdate=2021-05-30}}。 また旅館の若手経営者らが設立した[[合同会社]]ミライズが[[空き家]]を活用して、既存店舗と競合しない体験型店舗を相次ぎオープンした。第1弾は2014年オープンの「新潟[[地酒]] 蔵」で、その後も[[干物]]や[[発酵食品]]などを扱う「新潟地物 旨（うまみ）」（2015年）、[[せんべい]]を手焼きできる「新潟米菓 田（でん）」（2016年）、[[ワイン]]や[[雪室]]貯蔵した[[コーヒー]]を販売する「新潟飲物 香（かおり）」（2017年）、米粉商品の専門店「新潟米粉 米（べい）」（2018年）、スムージー&ジェラート専門店「新潟菜果 実（みのり）」（2019年）など続々とオープンした。2020年11月にはクラフトビール醸造所がオープンした{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=朝日新聞|date=2020-11-24}}。 こうしたまちづくりの取り組みは[[全国旅館ホテル生活衛生同業組合連合会]]（全旅連）が主催する「人に優しい地域の宿づくり賞」最高賞{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20200629552420.html|title=月岡温泉街の空き店舗再生評価 「人に優しい地域の宿づくり賞」で最高賞|publisher=[[新潟日報]]|date=2020-06-29}}や、コンパクトなまちづくり推進協議会などが主催する「先進的まちづくり大賞」国土交通大臣賞を受賞している。 このほか、温泉街にはカフェや[[駄菓子屋]]、[[足湯]]、昔ながらの饅頭屋や酒屋、寿司屋、居酒屋など様々な店舗が軒を連ねる。また、昭和初期に始まった[[芸妓]]が現在でも続いている{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20201102578613.html|title=足湯あったか 演舞あでやか 月岡芸妓、温泉盛り上げに一役|publisher=新潟日報|date=2020-11-02}}。 == 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉]]（弱アルカリ性 低張性 高温泉）[https://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ 月岡温泉について] - 月岡温泉観光協会.2018年12月23日閲覧。 * [[源泉]][[温度]]は約50度。 * 温泉は[[風呂|湯船]]で透き通ったエメラルドグリーン色を呈する 美肌に対する[[効能]]があるとされる。[[磐梯熱海温泉]]、[[いわき湯本温泉]]とともに「磐越三美人湯」に数えられる。 硫黄成分濃度の高さで知られ、その含有量は[[万座温泉]]に次ぐ日本で2番目の含有量である。硫黄泉としては珍しい弱アルカリ性であり、肌に優しい硫黄泉として多くの女性から親しまれてきた。また、塩化物温泉らしく温熱効果が高く、湯冷めにくい温泉として健康増進にも効果が高いとされている。
硫黄泉への入浴は、皮膚に効果的に働きかけ色白になる可能性も含み、入浴後、肌がつるつるになる事から「美人になれる温泉」と称されるようになる。 入浴剤と間違われるほど美しいエメラルドグリーン色を呈しているが、季節や天気などの気象条件により乳白色などに色が変化する場合もある。昔人は湯色を見て、翌日の天気を予想したと言われる。 == 周辺 == 温泉街に隣接して「[[月岡カリオンパーク]]」があり、ガラス作り体験施設や刀剣伝承館などを内包する。また、共同浴場「元湯 美人の泉」も設けられている。 2010年（平成22年）7月には、温泉街の南側に[[農産物直売所]]やイタリア料理店、ビュッフェ形式の農家レストラン、菓子店などからなる複合施設「月岡わくわくファーム」がオープン{{Cite news|title=農産物直売・伊料理・菓子、月岡温泉に複合施設、豊栄わくわく広場など。|newspaper=[[日本経済新聞]]|date=2010-07-28|page=22 地方経済面 新潟}}。2016年夏には同時に50人が乗れる[[ブランコ]]が設置され、「日本一長い木製ブランコ」として認定された{{Cite news|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20160911-M5SDVVOU5BMOHAOWRQIV5324T4/|title=甲信越ある記 新発田市月岡わくわくファーム 日本一長いブランコ|publisher=産経新聞|date=2016-09-11}}。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan17s3.jpg|thumb|250px|「月岡温泉発祥ノ地」の石碑]] 大正4年（1915年）に開湯{{Cite book|和書 |title =新潟温泉風土記 |publisher = 野島出版|author = 島津光夫 |date = 2001-06-11 |ref=風土記}}（一部の文献では大正6年とされている{{PDFlink|[https://www.niigataken-shokocho.jp/niigata/kyoudo/kfdk003.pdf 下越の名湯「月岡温泉」の今昔]}} - 新潟県小学校長会.2018年12月23日閲覧。{{Wayback|date= 20181224023804 |url= http://dspace.lib.niigata-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/10191/11526/1/10_29_0004.pdf |title= 新潟県における温泉開発の歴史 白石秀一.新潟応用地質研究会誌（2005年3月刊行）}}）。
現在の「月岡温泉発祥ノ地」の石碑付近の[[石油]]掘削中の井戸から50度を超える湯が湧き出し、本間周三郎氏がそこに共同浴場を建てたのが月岡温泉の始まりと言われている。共同浴場の隣に「青木館」が湯治宿を開業、続いて「浪花屋」をはじめとする湯治宿が数多く立ち並び、併せて「新喜久屋」などの自炊の為の商店も現れた（「旧湯」と称する）。 大正末～[[昭和]]初期に新しく温泉を掘り当て、現在の足湯付近に二階建ての共同浴場を中心とする新湯温泉団地が整備される（「新湯」と称する）。従来の旧湯と併せて湯治場としての月岡温泉が出来上がる。その後、近代的温泉地としての発展に向け豊富な湯量を求め掘削を進め、昭和32年、それまで1つだった内湯旅館を、25の旅館を内湯完備とした。昭和61年には全旅館が内湯完備となる。 昭和34年、現在のホテル華鳳付近に「月岡ヘルスセンター」と県内初の[[動物園]]「行楽苑」がオープン。その後「月岡ランド（大動植物園）と月岡ヘルスセンター」と改称した。園内のグリーンステージでは、多くの著名な歌手が歌い、[[山口百恵]]や[[ザ・ドリフターズ|ドリフターズ]]なども出演し活況を呈し、新潟県内外から来る多くの家族連れで賑わうようになる。現在でも唄われている『月岡小唄』や『月岡よさこい』は、この頃作られ、昭和初期より集まり始めた芸妓の数も昭和50年代には150名を超えた。 [[上越新幹線]]や高速道路の開通に合わせて「大越後かかしの祭典」など、様々な観光イベントが催され、団体旅行ブームもあり、平成10年度には年間入込数が78.5万人と月岡温泉開湯以降最大となる。 その後、団体旅行から個人旅行へ旅行志向も変化し、入込数は減少する。増加する個人旅行客へ向け、新潟県内の全酒蔵の酒が楽しめる「越後の酒天湯子」や「恋人の聖地プロジェクト」などを開催。「足湯広場」「石畳通り」の整備などの事業も進められている。近年では「世界の鍋グランプリ」や「[[SPA SONIC]]」なども開催されている。 == アクセス == ; 公共交通 * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[白新線]] [[豊栄駅]]南口より「月岡温泉シャトルバス」で約25分 - 新潟市街との直通路線バスが2003年秋に廃止になったことを受け、「北陸信越地域二次交通活性化プロジェクト」の一環として2004年7月に運行開始した{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper=日本経済新聞|date=2004-07-10|page=22 地方経済面 新潟}}。 * JR[[新発田駅]]より豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」（平日のみ運行）で約30分{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html |title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」 |accessdate=2022-03-10 |publisher=新発田市}}。 * JR[[西新発田駅]]より豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」（平日のみ運行）で約25分{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html |title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」 |accessdate=2022-03-10 |publisher=新発田市}}。 * JR羽越本線 [[月岡駅 (新潟県)|月岡駅]]よりタクシーで約10分　※旅館によっては無料送迎も有り * [[新潟空港]]から事前予約制バス「月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー」乗車（[[新発田城]]経由）{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=新潟空港|accessdate=2021-05-30}} * 東京（新宿・池袋）から[[泉観光バス (新潟県)|泉観光バス]]「'''NETWORK''' 821便」利用（土曜など特定日のみ運行）、「月岡温泉」下車[https://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - 泉観光バス.2018年12月23日閲覧。 ; 自動車 * [[日本海東北自動車道]]・[[豊栄新潟東港インターチェンジ|豊栄新潟東港IC]]より約20分 * [[磐越自動車道]]・[[安田インターチェンジ|安田IC]]より約20分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] - 温泉で開催される[[ロック・フェスティバル|音楽フェスティバル]] * [[月岡動物園]] * [[新潟県の観光地]] * [[豊富温泉]]、[[羽根沢温泉]]、[[瀬波温泉]] - 同じく石油採掘に関連した温泉。 == 外部リンク == * [https://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ 月岡温泉観光協会] ** {{PDFlink|[https://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf 月岡温泉街マップ]}} {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} [[Category:新潟県の温泉]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[月岡温泉]] to [[Tsukioka Onsen]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 月岡温泉 |画像 = 160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan02s3.jpg |コメント = 月岡温泉{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|marker=}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[新発田市]]月岡温泉 | 緯度度 = 37 | 緯度分 = 52 | 緯度秒 = 49 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 18 | 経度秒 = 33 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |交通アクセス = [[#アクセス|アクセス]]の項を参照 |泉質 = [[硫黄泉|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉]] |泉温 = 50 |湧出量 = |pH = 8 |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''月岡温泉'''（つきおかおんせん）は、[[新潟県]][[新発田市]]にある[[温泉]]。硫黄含有量の多さで知られる。温泉が所在する住所も「月岡温泉」となっている。 == 概要 == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan21s3.jpg|thumb|300px|温泉旅館が立ち並ぶ通り（2016年7月）]] 新潟県を代表する[[温泉地]]であり、[[にっぽんの温泉100選]]に例年ランクインする（2020年度は総合ランキングで全国16位{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430020121900701.pdf|title=第34回「にっぽんの温泉100選」全ランキング|publisher=[[観光経済新聞]]|date=2020-12-19|page=7}}、泉質で同8位{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430021010101501.pdf|title=第34回にっぽんの温泉100選 選んだ理由別順位|publisher=観光経済新聞|date=2021-01-01|page=15}}）。[[旅館]]・[[ホテル]]（月岡温泉観光協会ウェブサイト掲載施設は12軒{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hotel/|title=月岡温泉内の旅館ホテル一覧|publisher=月岡温泉観光協会|accessdate=2021-05-30}}）と[[商店街]]からなる[[温泉街]]がある。 [[旬刊旅行新聞|旅行新聞新社]]主催の「プロが選ぶ日本のホテル・旅館100選」で毎年上位に選出される日本を代表する大旅館から、宿泊予約サイト「[[楽天トラベル]]」からアワードやマイスターに選ばれた旅館、観劇やプロレスなどを開催する旅館、昔ながらの[[湯治]]を楽しめる旅館まで、様々な楽しみ方が出来る[[温泉地]]。[[大正]]4年（1915年）の開湯より100年を控えた2014年前後から各種の催しが開催され、近年は観光協会を中心としたものから、各旅館や商店が行うものまで「町おこし」が盛んになっている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title= 平成28年度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書 |publisher= 新潟県観光局 観光企画課|accessdate= 2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=にいがた経済新聞|date=2018-09-10}}。 == 温泉街 == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|温泉街（2016年7月）]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|「月あかりの庭」]] [[越後平野]]東縁に位置し、「新潟の奥座敷」として脚光を浴びていた温泉地だが、[[バブル経済]]期に年120万人いた観光客数は[[バブル崩壊]]後に半減し、旅館も3分の2が廃業・閉鎖された{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100年空き家で米・酒|newspaper=[[日経MJ]] 観光・インバウンド面|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=まち交ネット|accessdate=2020-07-05}}。また、旅館の大型化や旅館の自社施設内での顧客囲い込みが進んだため温泉街の役割が低下し、一時は「温泉以外は何もない」と言われた。 その後、「歩きたくなる温泉街」を目指して再生の取り組みが行われ、上記の開湯100年に合わせて、景観整備や店舗リノベーションの取り組みが続々と行われた。 2014年には観光協会が、荒れ地になっていた温泉発祥地を[[飲泉]]もできる「[[源泉]]の杜」として整備。さらに商店街の外観をなるべく茶色にする景観改善が行われた。2018年には、旅館跡の空き地に和風庭園「月あかりの庭」が設けられた{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228038.pdf|title=平成30年度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=新潟県観光局 観光企画課|accessdate=2021-05-30}}。 また旅館の若手経営者らが設立した[[合同会社]]ミライズが[[空き家]]を活用して、既存店舗と競合しない体験型店舗を相次ぎオープンした。第1弾は2014年オープンの「新潟[[地酒]] 蔵」で、その後も[[干物]]や[[発酵食品]]などを扱う「新潟地物 旨（うまみ）」（2015年）、[[せんべい]]を手焼きできる「新潟米菓 田（でん）」（2016年）、[[ワイン]]や[[雪室]]貯蔵した[[コーヒー]]を販売する「新潟飲物 香（かおり）」（2017年）、米粉商品の専門店「新潟米粉 米（べい）」（2018年）、スムージー&ジェラート専門店「新潟菜果 実（みのり）」（2019年）など続々とオープンした。2020年11月にはクラフトビール醸造所がオープンした{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=朝日新聞|date=2020-11-24}}。 こうしたまちづくりの取り組みは[[全国旅館ホテル生活衛生同業組合連合会]]（全旅連）が主催する「人に優しい地域の宿づくり賞」最高賞{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20200629552420.html|title=月岡温泉街の空き店舗再生評価 「人に優しい地域の宿づくり賞」で最高賞|publisher=[[新潟日報]]|date=2020-06-29}}や、コンパクトなまちづくり推進協議会などが主催する「先進的まちづくり大賞」国土交通大臣賞を受賞している。 このほか、温泉街にはカフェや[[駄菓子屋]]、[[足湯]]、昔ながらの饅頭屋や酒屋、寿司屋、居酒屋など様々な店舗が軒を連ねる。また、昭和初期に始まった[[芸妓]]が現在でも続いている{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20201102578613.html|title=足湯あったか 演舞あでやか 月岡芸妓、温泉盛り上げに一役|publisher=新潟日報|date=2020-11-02}}。 == 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉]]（弱アルカリ性 低張性 高温泉）[https://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ 月岡温泉について] - 月岡温泉観光協会.2018年12月23日閲覧。 * [[源泉]][[温度]]は約50度。 * 温泉は[[風呂|湯船]]で透き通ったエメラルドグリーン色を呈する 美肌に対する[[効能]]があるとされる。[[磐梯熱海温泉]]、[[いわき湯本温泉]]とともに「磐越三美人湯」に数えられる。 硫黄成分濃度の高さで知られ、その含有量は[[万座温泉]]に次ぐ日本で2番目の含有量である。硫黄泉としては珍しい弱アルカリ性であり、肌に優しい硫黄泉として多くの女性から親しまれてきた。また、塩化物温泉らしく温熱効果が高く、湯冷めにくい温泉として健康増進にも効果が高いとされている。
硫黄泉への入浴は、皮膚に効果的に働きかけ色白になる可能性も含み、入浴後、肌がつるつるになる事から「美人になれる温泉」と称されるようになる。 入浴剤と間違われるほど美しいエメラルドグリーン色を呈しているが、季節や天気などの気象条件により乳白色などに色が変化する場合もある。昔人は湯色を見て、翌日の天気を予想したと言われる。 == 周辺 == 温泉街に隣接して「[[月岡カリオンパーク]]」があり、ガラス作り体験施設や刀剣伝承館などを内包する。また、共同浴場「元湯 美人の泉」も設けられている。 2010年（平成22年）7月には、温泉街の南側に[[農産物直売所]]やイタリア料理店、ビュッフェ形式の農家レストラン、菓子店などからなる複合施設「月岡わくわくファーム」がオープン{{Cite news|title=農産物直売・伊料理・菓子、月岡温泉に複合施設、豊栄わくわく広場など。|newspaper=[[日本経済新聞]]|date=2010-07-28|page=22 地方経済面 新潟}}。2016年夏には同時に50人が乗れる[[ブランコ]]が設置され、「日本一長い木製ブランコ」として認定された{{Cite news|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20160911-M5SDVVOU5BMOHAOWRQIV5324T4/|title=甲信越ある記 新発田市月岡わくわくファーム 日本一長いブランコ|publisher=産経新聞|date=2016-09-11}}。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan17s3.jpg|thumb|250px|「月岡温泉発祥ノ地」の石碑]] 大正4年（1915年）に開湯{{Cite book|和書 |title =新潟温泉風土記 |publisher = 野島出版|author = 島津光夫 |date = 2001-06-11 |ref=風土記}}（一部の文献では大正6年とされている{{PDFlink|[https://www.niigataken-shokocho.jp/niigata/kyoudo/kfdk003.pdf 下越の名湯「月岡温泉」の今昔]}} - 新潟県小学校長会.2018年12月23日閲覧。{{Wayback|date= 20181224023804 |url= http://dspace.lib.niigata-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/10191/11526/1/10_29_0004.pdf |title= 新潟県における温泉開発の歴史 白石秀一.新潟応用地質研究会誌（2005年3月刊行）}}）。
現在の「月岡温泉発祥ノ地」の石碑付近の[[石油]]掘削中の井戸から50度を超える湯が湧き出し、本間周三郎氏がそこに共同浴場を建てたのが月岡温泉の始まりと言われている。共同浴場の隣に「青木館」が湯治宿を開業、続いて「浪花屋」をはじめとする湯治宿が数多く立ち並び、併せて「新喜久屋」などの自炊の為の商店も現れた（「旧湯」と称する）。 大正末～[[昭和]]初期に新しく温泉を掘り当て、現在の足湯付近に二階建ての共同浴場を中心とする新湯温泉団地が整備される（「新湯」と称する）。従来の旧湯と併せて湯治場としての月岡温泉が出来上がる。その後、近代的温泉地としての発展に向け豊富な湯量を求め掘削を進め、昭和32年、それまで1つだった内湯旅館を、25の旅館を内湯完備とした。昭和61年には全旅館が内湯完備となる。 昭和34年、現在のホテル華鳳付近に「月岡ヘルスセンター」と県内初の[[動物園]]「行楽苑」がオープン。その後「月岡ランド（大動植物園）と月岡ヘルスセンター」と改称した。園内のグリーンステージでは、多くの著名な歌手が歌い、[[山口百恵]]や[[ザ・ドリフターズ|ドリフターズ]]なども出演し活況を呈し、新潟県内外から来る多くの家族連れで賑わうようになる。現在でも唄われている『月岡小唄』や『月岡よさこい』は、この頃作られ、昭和初期より集まり始めた芸妓の数も昭和50年代には150名を超えた。 [[上越新幹線]]や高速道路の開通に合わせて「大越後かかしの祭典」など、様々な観光イベントが催され、団体旅行ブームもあり、平成10年度には年間入込数が78.5万人と月岡温泉開湯以降最大となる。 その後、団体旅行から個人旅行へ旅行志向も変化し、入込数は減少する。増加する個人旅行客へ向け、新潟県内の全酒蔵の酒が楽しめる「越後の酒天湯子」や「恋人の聖地プロジェクト」などを開催。「足湯広場」「石畳通り」の整備などの事業も進められている。近年では「世界の鍋グランプリ」や「[[SPA SONIC]]」なども開催されている。 == アクセス == ; 公共交通 * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[白新線]] [[豊栄駅]]南口より「月岡温泉シャトルバス」で約25分 - 新潟市街との直通路線バスが2003年秋に廃止になったことを受け、「北陸信越地域二次交通活性化プロジェクト」の一環として2004年7月に運行開始した{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper=日本経済新聞|date=2004-07-10|page=22 地方経済面 新潟}}。 * JR[[新発田駅]]より豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」（平日のみ運行）で約30分{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html |title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」 |accessdate=2022-03-10 |publisher=新発田市}}。 * JR[[西新発田駅]]より豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」（平日のみ運行）で約25分{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html |title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」 |accessdate=2022-03-10 |publisher=新発田市}}。 * JR羽越本線 [[月岡駅 (新潟県)|月岡駅]]よりタクシーで約10分　※旅館によっては無料送迎も有り * [[新潟空港]]から事前予約制バス「月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー」乗車（[[新発田城]]経由）{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=新潟空港|accessdate=2021-05-30}} * 東京（新宿・池袋）から[[泉観光バス (新潟県)|泉観光バス]]「'''NETWORK''' 821便」利用（土曜など特定日のみ運行）、「月岡温泉」下車[https://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - 泉観光バス.2018年12月23日閲覧。 ; 自動車 * [[日本海東北自動車道]]・[[豊栄新潟東港インターチェンジ|豊栄新潟東港IC]]より約20分 * [[磐越自動車道]]・[[安田インターチェンジ|安田IC]]より約20分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] - 温泉で開催される[[ロック・フェスティバル|音楽フェスティバル]] * [[月岡動物園]] * [[新潟県の観光地]] * [[豊富温泉]]、[[羽根沢温泉]]、[[瀬波温泉]] - 同じく石油採掘に関連した温泉。 == 外部リンク == * [https://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ 月岡温泉観光協会] ** {{PDFlink|[https://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf 月岡温泉街マップ]}} {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} [[Category:新潟県の温泉]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsukioka Onsen |image = 160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan02s3.jpg |Comment = Tsukioka Onsen{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|marker=}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tsukioka Onsen |Latitude Degrees = 37 |Latitude Minutes = 52 |Latitude Seconds = 49 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 18 |Longitude Seconds = 33 | E/W = E |Access = See the "Access" section |Water Type = {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing Sodium Chloride Hot Spring|lt_ja=含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Temperature = 50 |Discharge = |pH = 8 |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }} '''Tsukioka Onsen''' (月岡温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is renowned for its high sulfur content—the very address of the hot spring is designated "Tsukioka Onsen." == Overview == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan21s3.jpg|thumb|300px|A street lined with hot spring inns (photographed in July 2016)]] Tsukioka Onsen is one of the premier hot spring resorts of Niigata Prefecture and is annually Ranked among "Japan's 100 Hot Springs" (in 2020, it Ranked 16th overall and 8th for water quality{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430020121900701.pdf|title=第34回「にっぽんの温泉100選」全ランキング|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2020-12-19|page=7}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430021010101501.pdf|title=第34回にっぽんの温泉100選 選んだ理由別順位|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2021-01-01|page=15}}). The onsen town comprises a district of inns and hotels (the Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association website lists 12 such establishments{{Cite web|url=http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hotel/|title=月岡温泉内の旅館ホテル一覧|publisher=Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association|accessdate=2021-05-30}}) along with a shopping street. A variety of experiences are available here—from grand traditional inns frequently chosen in "Japan’s 100 Best Hotels and Inns" by leading travel newspaper sponsors, to establishments awarded by online reservation sites like {{ill|Rakuten Travel|ja|楽天トラベル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to venues offering theatrical and professional wrestling events, and even inns where visitors can enjoy traditional therapeutic bathing (湯治). Since its opening in Taishō 4 (1915), and especially from around 2014 when events were staged to mark its 100th anniversary, a host of revitalization initiatives have been implemented, ranging from efforts led by the tourism association to those undertaken by individual inns and shops.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title=平成28th year 度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=Niigata Prefecture Tourism Bureau, Tourism Planning Division|accessdate=2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=Niigata Keizai Shimbun|date=2018-09-10}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|The onsen town (photographed in July 2016)]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|"Tsukiakari no Niwa" (Garden of Moonlight)]] Located on the eastern edge of the Echigo Plain, Tsukioka Onsen once shone as "Niigata’s inner sanctuary." However, during the bubble economy its annual visitor count reached 1.2 million, and following the bubble collapse, visitor numbers were halved and two-thirds of its inns closed down{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100th year 空き家で米・酒|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei MJ|ja|日経MJ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=Machikou Net|accessdate=2020-07-05}}. Furthermore, trends toward larger inns and in-house customer retention diminished the traditional role of the onsen town, once leading some to claim that "there is nothing here but the hot spring.". In response, efforts to revitalize Tsukioka Onsen into an onsen town that invites leisurely strolls have been implemented. In conjunction with its 100th anniversary, initiatives for landscape improvement and store renovation have been vigorously pursued. In 2014, the tourism association redeveloped the once-neglected birthplace of the hot spring into "Gensen no Mori," a park where visitors can sample the hot spring water. Additionally, the shopping street's exterior was revamped with a brown-toned aesthetic. In 2018, a traditional Japanese garden, "Tsukiakari no Niwa," was established on a vacant lot formerly occupied by an inn. Moreover, a company named Miraizu, founded by young inn managers, has repurposed vacant houses to open a series of experiential stores that complement rather than compete with existing shops. The first such venture, "Niigata Jizake Kura" (a sake warehouse), opened in 2014, followed by "Niigata Jimono Umami" (dealing in local dried fish and fermented foods, 2015), "Niigata Rice Snack Den" (offering hand-roasted senbei, 2016), "Niigata Inbutsu Kaori" (selling wine and coffee stored in snow cellars, 2017), "Niigata Rice Flour Bei" (a specialty shop for rice flour products, 2018), and "Niigata Saika Minori" (a smoothie and gelato specialty store, 2019). In November 2020, a craft beer brewery also opened{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|date=2020-11-24}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sodium Chloride Hot Spring with Sulfur|ja|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) (weakly alkaline, low tonicity, high-temperature hot spring)[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ About Tsukioka Onsen] - Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association. Accessed December 23, 2018. * The source temperature is approximately 50℃. * The hot spring water in the bathtubs exhibits a striking emerald green transparency and is reputed to have beautifying effects. It is counted among the "Iban-e Three Beautiful Hot Springs" along with {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its sulfur content is notably high—second only to {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Japan. Unusually for a sulfur spring, it is weakly alkaline and gentle on the skin. Moreover, its chloride properties confer a high thermal effect, making the hot spring less prone to cooling and thus highly beneficial for health.
Bathing in this sulfur spring is believed to have an effective beautifying impact on the skin, potentially leading to a lighter complexion, and leaving the skin exceptionally smooth—hence its reputation as the "hot spring that can make you beautiful." Although its water is a vivid emerald green that might be mistaken for a bath additive, its color may shift to milky white under certain seasonal or weather conditions. It is said that in the past, people would predict the next day’s weather by observing the hue of the hot spring water. == Surroundings == Adjacent to the onsen town is the {{ill|Tsukioka Kari-on Park|ja|月岡カリオンパーク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which houses a glassmaking experience facility and a sword heritage hall. Additionally, a public bath, "Moto-yu Bijin no Izumi" (Origin of the Beautiful Spring), is available. == History == A stone monument marking the "Birthplace of Tsukioka Onsen" stands near a drilling well from which water over 50℃ flows, and it is said that Shūzō Honma built a public bath there, marking the inception of Tsukioka Onsen. Adjacent to this public bath, "Aoki-kan" opened as a therapeutic lodging; subsequently, numerous therapeutic inns such as "Naniwaya" emerged, along with self-catering shops like "Shinkikuyā" (collectively referred to as the "Old Yu"). In the late Taishō to early Shōwa periods, a new hot spring district was developed near the current footbath area, centered on a two-story public bath (referred to as the "New Yu"). Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] [[Category:New]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|月岡温泉|version=104109443|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsukioka Onsen |image = 160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan02s3.jpg |Comment = Tsukioka Onsen{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|marker=}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tsukioka Onsen |Latitude Degrees = 37 |Latitude Minutes = 52 |Latitude Seconds = 49 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 18 |Longitude Seconds = 33 | E/W = E |Access = See the "Access" section |Water Type = {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing Sodium Chloride Hot Spring|lt_ja=含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Temperature = 50 |Discharge = |pH = 8 |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }} '''Tsukioka Onsen''' (月岡温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is renowned for its high sulfur content—the very address of the hot spring is designated "Tsukioka Onsen." == Overview == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan21s3.jpg|thumb|300px|A street lined with hot spring inns (photographed in July 2016)]] Tsukioka Onsen is one of the premier hot spring resorts of Niigata Prefecture and is annually Ranked among "Japan's 100 Hot Springs" (in 2020, it Ranked 16th overall and 8th for water quality{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430020121900701.pdf|title=第34回「にっぽんの温泉100選」全ランキング|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2020-12-19|page=7}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430021010101501.pdf|title=第34回にっぽんの温泉100選 選んだ理由別順位|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2021-01-01|page=15}}). The onsen town comprises a district of inns and hotels (the Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association website lists 12 such establishments{{Cite web|url=http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hotel/|title=月岡温泉内の旅館ホテル一覧|publisher=Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association|accessdate=2021-05-30}}) along with a shopping street. A variety of experiences are available here—from grand traditional inns frequently chosen in "Japan’s 100 Best Hotels and Inns" by leading travel newspaper sponsors, to establishments awarded by online reservation sites like {{ill|Rakuten Travel|ja|楽天トラベル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to venues offering theatrical and professional wrestling events, and even inns where visitors can enjoy traditional therapeutic bathing (湯治). Since its opening in Taishō 4 (1915), and especially from around 2014 when events were staged to mark its 100th anniversary, a host of revitalization initiatives have been implemented, ranging from efforts led by the tourism association to those undertaken by individual inns and shops.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title=平成28th year 度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=Niigata Prefecture Tourism Bureau, Tourism Planning Division|accessdate=2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=Niigata Keizai Shimbun|date=2018-09-10}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|The onsen town (photographed in July 2016)]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|"Tsukiakari no Niwa" (Garden of Moonlight)]] Located on the eastern edge of the Echigo Plain, Tsukioka Onsen once shone as "Niigata’s inner sanctuary." However, during the bubble economy its annual visitor count reached 1.2 million, and following the bubble collapse, visitor numbers were halved and two-thirds of its inns closed down{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100th year 空き家で米・酒|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei MJ|ja|日経MJ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=Machikou Net|accessdate=2020-07-05}}. Furthermore, trends toward larger inns and in-house customer retention diminished the traditional role of the onsen town, once leading some to claim that "there is nothing here but the hot spring.". In response, efforts to revitalize Tsukioka Onsen into an onsen town that invites leisurely strolls have been implemented. In conjunction with its 100th anniversary, initiatives for landscape improvement and store renovation have been vigorously pursued. In 2014, the tourism association redeveloped the once-neglected birthplace of the hot spring into "Gensen no Mori," a park where visitors can sample the hot spring water. Additionally, the shopping street's exterior was revamped with a brown-toned aesthetic. In 2018, a traditional Japanese garden, "Tsukiakari no Niwa," was established on a vacant lot formerly occupied by an inn. Moreover, a company named Miraizu, founded by young inn managers, has repurposed vacant houses to open a series of experiential stores that complement rather than compete with existing shops. The first such venture, "Niigata Jizake Kura" (a sake warehouse), opened in 2014, followed by "Niigata Jimono Umami" (dealing in local dried fish and fermented foods, 2015), "Niigata Rice Snack Den" (offering hand-roasted senbei, 2016), "Niigata Inbutsu Kaori" (selling wine and coffee stored in snow cellars, 2017), "Niigata Rice Flour Bei" (a specialty shop for rice flour products, 2018), and "Niigata Saika Minori" (a smoothie and gelato specialty store, 2019). In November 2020, a craft beer brewery also opened{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|date=2020-11-24}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sodium Chloride Hot Spring with Sulfur|ja|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) (weakly alkaline, low tonicity, high-temperature hot spring)[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ About Tsukioka Onsen] - Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association. Accessed December 23, 2018. * The source temperature is approximately 50℃. * The hot spring water in the bathtubs exhibits a striking emerald green transparency and is reputed to have beautifying effects. It is counted among the "Iban-e Three Beautiful Hot Springs" along with {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its sulfur content is notably high—second only to {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Japan. Unusually for a sulfur spring, it is weakly alkaline and gentle on the skin. Moreover, its chloride properties confer a high thermal effect, making the hot spring less prone to cooling and thus highly beneficial for health.
Bathing in this sulfur spring is believed to have an effective beautifying impact on the skin, potentially leading to a lighter complexion, and leaving the skin exceptionally smooth—hence its reputation as the "hot spring that can make you beautiful." Although its water is a vivid emerald green that might be mistaken for a bath additive, its color may shift to milky white under certain seasonal or weather conditions. It is said that in the past, people would predict the next day’s weather by observing the hue of the hot spring water. == Surroundings == Adjacent to the onsen town is the {{ill|Tsukioka Kari-on Park|ja|月岡カリオンパーク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which houses a glassmaking experience facility and a sword heritage hall. Additionally, a public bath, "Moto-yu Bijin no Izumi" (Origin of the Beautiful Spring), is available. == History == A stone monument marking the "Birthplace of Tsukioka Onsen" stands near a drilling well from which water over 50℃ flows, and it is said that Shūzō Honma built a public bath there, marking the inception of Tsukioka Onsen. Adjacent to this public bath, "Aoki-kan" opened as a therapeutic lodging; subsequently, numerous therapeutic inns such as "Naniwaya" emerged, along with self-catering shops like "Shinkikuyā" (collectively referred to as the "Old Yu"). In the late Taishō to early Shōwa periods, a new hot spring district was developed near the current footbath area, centered on a two-story public bath (referred to as the "New Yu"). Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきおかおんせん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|月岡温泉|version=104109443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] [[Category:New]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]]

Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR {{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsukioka Onsen |image = 160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan02s3.jpg |Comment = Tsukioka Onsen{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|marker=}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tsukioka Onsen |Latitude Degrees = 37 |Latitude Minutes = 52 |Latitude Seconds = 49 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 18 |Longitude Seconds = 33 | E/W = E |Access = See the "Access" section |Water Type = {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing Sodium Chloride Hot Spring|lt_ja=含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Temperature = 50 |Discharge = |pH = 8 |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }} '''Tsukioka Onsen''' (月岡温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata City|ja|新発田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is renowned for its high sulfur content—the very address of the hot spring is designated "Tsukioka Onsen." == Overview == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan21s3.jpg|thumb|300px|A street lined with hot spring inns (photographed in July 2016)]] Tsukioka Onsen is one of the premier hot spring resorts of Niigata Prefecture and is annually Ranked among "Japan's 100 Hot Springs" (in 2020, it Ranked 16th overall and 8th for water quality{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430020121900701.pdf|title=第34回「にっぽんの温泉100選」全ランキング|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2020-12-19|page=7}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.kankokeizai.com/image/2020/OK0430021010101501.pdf|title=第34回にっぽんの温泉100選 選んだ理由別順位|publisher={{ill|Kankō Keizai Shimbun|ja|観光経済新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2021-01-01|page=15}}). The onsen town comprises a district of inns and hotels (the Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association website lists 12 such establishments{{Cite web|url=http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hotel/|title=月岡温泉内の旅館ホテル一覧|publisher=Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association|accessdate=2021-05-30}}) along with a shopping street. A variety of experiences are available here—from grand traditional inns frequently chosen in "Japan’s 100 Best Hotels and Inns" by leading travel newspaper sponsors, to establishments awarded by online reservation sites like {{ill|Rakuten Travel|ja|楽天トラベル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to venues offering theatrical and professional wrestling events, and even inns where visitors can enjoy traditional therapeutic bathing (湯治). Since its opening in Taishō 4 (1915), and especially from around 2014 when events were staged to mark its 100th anniversary, a host of revitalization initiatives have been implemented, ranging from efforts led by the tourism association to those undertaken by individual inns and shops.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title=平成28th year 度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=Niigata Prefecture Tourism Bureau, Tourism Planning Division|accessdate=2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=Niigata Keizai Shimbun|date=2018-09-10}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|The onsen town (photographed in July 2016)]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|"Tsukiakari no Niwa" (Garden of Moonlight)]] Located on the eastern edge of the Echigo Plain, Tsukioka Onsen once shone as "Niigata’s inner sanctuary." However, during the bubble economy its annual visitor count reached 1.2 million, and following the bubble collapse, visitor numbers were halved and two-thirds of its inns closed down{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100th year 空き家で米・酒|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei MJ|ja|日経MJ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=Machikou Net|accessdate=2020-07-05}}. Furthermore, trends toward larger inns and in-house customer retention diminished the traditional role of the onsen town, once leading some to claim that "there is nothing here but the hot spring.". In response, efforts to revitalize Tsukioka Onsen into an onsen town that invites leisurely strolls have been implemented. In conjunction with its 100th anniversary, initiatives for landscape improvement and store renovation have been vigorously pursued. In 2014, the tourism association redeveloped the once-neglected birthplace of the hot spring into "Gensen no Mori," a park where visitors can sample the hot spring water. Additionally, the shopping street's exterior was revamped with a brown-toned aesthetic. In 2018, a traditional Japanese garden, "Tsukiakari no Niwa," was established on a vacant lot formerly occupied by an inn. Moreover, a company named Miraizu, founded by young inn managers, has repurposed vacant houses to open a series of experiential stores that complement rather than compete with existing shops. The first such venture, "Niigata Jizake Kura" (a sake warehouse), opened in 2014, followed by "Niigata Jimono Umami" (dealing in local dried fish and fermented foods, 2015), "Niigata Rice Snack Den" (offering hand-roasted senbei, 2016), "Niigata Inbutsu Kaori" (selling wine and coffee stored in snow cellars, 2017), "Niigata Rice Flour Bei" (a specialty shop for rice flour products, 2018), and "Niigata Saika Minori" (a smoothie and gelato specialty store, 2019). In November 2020, a craft beer brewery also opened{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|date=2020-11-24}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sodium Chloride Hot Spring with Sulfur|ja|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) (weakly alkaline, low tonicity, high-temperature hot spring)[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ About Tsukioka Onsen] - Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association. Accessed December 23, 2018. * The source temperature is approximately 50℃. * The hot spring water in the bathtubs exhibits a striking emerald green transparency and is reputed to have beautifying effects. It is counted among the "Iban-e Three Beautiful Hot Springs" along with {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its sulfur content is notably high—second only to {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Japan. Unusually for a sulfur spring, it is weakly alkaline and gentle on the skin. Moreover, its chloride properties confer a high thermal effect, making the hot spring less prone to cooling and thus highly beneficial for health.
Bathing in this sulfur spring is believed to have an effective beautifying impact on the skin, potentially leading to a lighter complexion, and leaving the skin exceptionally smooth—hence its reputation as the "hot spring that can make you beautiful." Although its water is a vivid emerald green that might be mistaken for a bath additive, its color may shift to milky white under certain seasonal or weather conditions. It is said that in the past, people would predict the next day’s weather by observing the hue of the hot spring water. == Surroundings == Adjacent to the onsen town is the {{ill|Tsukioka Kari-on Park|ja|月岡カリオンパーク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which houses a glassmaking experience facility and a sword heritage hall. Additionally, a public bath, "Moto-yu Bijin no Izumi" (Origin of the Beautiful Spring), is available. == History == A stone monument marking the "Birthplace of Tsukioka Onsen" stands near a drilling well from which water over 50℃ flows, and it is said that Shūzō Honma built a public bath there, marking the inception of Tsukioka Onsen. Adjacent to this public bath, "Aoki-kan" opened as a therapeutic lodging; subsequently, numerous therapeutic inns such as "Naniwaya" emerged, along with self-catering shops like "Shinkikuyā" (collectively referred to as the "Old Yu"). In the late Taishō to early Shōwa periods, a new hot spring district was developed near the current footbath area, centered on a two-story public bath (referred to as the "New Yu"). Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR Since its opening in Taishō 4 (1915), and especially from around 2014 when events were staged to mark its 100th anniversary, a host of revitalization initiatives have been implemented, ranging from efforts led by the tourism association to those undertaken by individual inns and shops.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/228040.pdf|title=平成28th year 度温泉地等魅力向上支援事業 事業報告書|publisher=Niigata Prefecture Tourism Bureau, Tourism Planning Division|accessdate=2021-05-30}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.niikei.jp/4090/|title=温泉街の賑わい創出のための取り組みが進む月岡温泉|publisher=Niigata Keizai Shimbun|date=2018-09-10}}. == Onsen Town == [[File:160717 Tsukioka Onsen Shibata Niigata pref Japan12s3.jpg|thumb|250px|The onsen town (photographed in July 2016)]] [[File:Tsukioka Onsen Tsukiakari May2020.jpg|thumb|250px|"Tsukiakari no Niwa" (Garden of Moonlight)]] Located on the eastern edge of the Echigo Plain, Tsukioka Onsen once shone as "Niigata’s inner sanctuary." However, during the bubble economy its annual visitor count reached 1.2 million, and following the bubble collapse, visitor numbers were halved and two-thirds of its inns closed down{{Cite news|title=【おもてなし魅せどころ】月岡温泉（新潟県新発田市）開湯100th year 空き家で米・酒|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei MJ|ja|日経MJ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2018-08-06}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.machikou-net.org/public/senshin_taisyou/file/file_05.pdf|title=第1回先進的まちづくり大賞 国土交通大臣賞 受賞者：合同会社ミライズ|publisher=Machikou Net|accessdate=2020-07-05}}. Furthermore, trends toward larger inns and in-house customer retention diminished the traditional role of the onsen town, once leading some to claim that "there is nothing here but the hot spring.". In response, efforts to revitalize Tsukioka Onsen into an onsen town that invites leisurely strolls have been implemented. In conjunction with its 100th anniversary, initiatives for landscape improvement and store renovation have been vigorously pursued. In 2014, the tourism association redeveloped the once-neglected birthplace of the hot spring into "Gensen no Mori," a park where visitors can sample the hot spring water. Additionally, the shopping street's exterior was revamped with a brown-toned aesthetic. In 2018, a traditional Japanese garden, "Tsukiakari no Niwa," was established on a vacant lot formerly occupied by an inn. Moreover, a company named Miraizu, founded by young inn managers, has repurposed vacant houses to open a series of experiential stores that complement rather than compete with existing shops. The first such venture, "Niigata Jizake Kura" (a sake warehouse), opened in 2014, followed by "Niigata Jimono Umami" (dealing in local dried fish and fermented foods, 2015), "Niigata Rice Snack Den" (offering hand-roasted senbei, 2016), "Niigata Inbutsu Kaori" (selling wine and coffee stored in snow cellars, 2017), "Niigata Rice Flour Bei" (a specialty shop for rice flour products, 2018), and "Niigata Saika Minori" (a smoothie and gelato specialty store, 2019). In November 2020, a craft beer brewery also opened{{Cite news|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASNCR6VH9NCCUOHB007.html|title=月岡温泉にクラフトビール醸造所 13日オープン|publisher=Asahi Shimbun|date=2020-11-24}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Sulfur Hot Spring|ja|硫黄泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sodium Chloride Hot Spring with Sulfur|ja|含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) (weakly alkaline, low tonicity, high-temperature hot spring)[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/hot-springs/ About Tsukioka Onsen] - Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association. Accessed December 23, 2018. * The source temperature is approximately 50℃. * The hot spring water in the bathtubs exhibits a striking emerald green transparency and is reputed to have beautifying effects. It is counted among the "Iban-e Three Beautiful Hot Springs" along with {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|ja|いわき湯本温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its sulfur content is notably high—second only to {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Japan. Unusually for a sulfur spring, it is weakly alkaline and gentle on the skin. Moreover, its chloride properties confer a high thermal effect, making the hot spring less prone to cooling and thus highly beneficial for health.
Bathing in this sulfur spring is believed to have an effective beautifying impact on the skin, potentially leading to a lighter complexion, and leaving the skin exceptionally smooth—hence its reputation as the "hot spring that can make you beautiful." Although its water is a vivid emerald green that might be mistaken for a bath additive, its color may shift to milky white under certain seasonal or weather conditions. It is said that in the past, people would predict the next day’s weather by observing the hue of the hot spring water. == Surroundings == Adjacent to the onsen town is the {{ill|Tsukioka Kari-on Park|ja|月岡カリオンパーク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which houses a glassmaking experience facility and a sword heritage hall. Additionally, a public bath, "Moto-yu Bijin no Izumi" (Origin of the Beautiful Spring), is available. == History == A stone monument marking the "Birthplace of Tsukioka Onsen" stands near a drilling well from which water over 50℃ flows, and it is said that Shūzō Honma built a public bath there, marking the inception of Tsukioka Onsen. Adjacent to this public bath, "Aoki-kan" opened as a therapeutic lodging; subsequently, numerous therapeutic inns such as "Naniwaya" emerged, along with self-catering shops like "Shinkikuyā" (collectively referred to as the "Old Yu"). In the late Taishō to early Shōwa periods, a new hot spring district was developed near the current footbath area, centered on a two-story public bath (referred to as the "New Yu"). Combined with the traditional Old Yu, Tsukioka Onsen evolved into a comprehensive therapeutic bathing area. Later, with further drilling to secure abundant hot water, the single inn with an indoor bath expanded to 25 inns all equipped with indoor baths by Shōwa 32, and by Shōwa 61 every inn had its own indoor bath. In Shōwa 34, near the present Hotel Kaho, the "Tsukioka Health Center" and the prefecture’s first zoo, "Tsukioka Land," were inaugurated. The park’s Green Stage hosted performances by many renowned singers, including {{ill|Yamaguchi Momoe|ja|山口百恵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and The Drifters, attracting numerous families from within and outside Niigata Prefecture. The songs "Tsukioka Ko-uta" and "Tsukioka Yosakoi," still sung today, originated during this period, and the number of geisha gathering in Tsukioka Onsen exceeded 150 by the 1950s. The opening of the {{ill|Joetsu Shinkansen|ja|上越新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and highways spurred various tourism events such as the "Ōetsugo Kakashi Festival," and during the group travel boom, overnight guest numbers reached 785,000 in fiscal 1998—the highest since Tsukioka Onsen opened. Subsequently, as travel trends shifted from group tours to individual travel, overnight guest numbers declined. To cater to the increasing number of individual travelers, events such as the "Echigo Sake Amayu-ko," which features sake from all Niigata breweries, and the "Lovers’ Holy Land Project" have been organized. Additionally, projects to develop a footbath plaza ("Ashiyu Hiroba") and to renovate Ishidatami Street have been implemented. In recent years, events such as the "World Nabe Grand Prix" and "SPA SONIC" have also been held. == Access == ; Public Transportation * From the south exit of {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Line|ja|白新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Toyoe Station|ja|豊栄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the "Tsukioka Onsen Shuttle Bus" for about 25 minutes – this direct route to Niigata City began operation in July 2004 as part of the "Secondary Traffic Revitalization Project for the Hokuriku-Shinetsu Region."{{Cite news|title=新潟交通北、月岡温泉―JR豊栄駅、シャトルバスを運行。|newspaper={{ill|Nikkei Shimbun|ja|日本経済新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=2004-07-10|page=22}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shibata Station|ja|新発田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the Toyo Regional Community Bus "Honda-Tennō-go" (operates on weekdays only) for about 30 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nishishibata Station|ja|西新発田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the same community bus "Honda-Tennō-go" for about 25 minutes{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shibata.lg.jp/kurashi/kotsu/bus/1017258/1017259.html|title=豊浦地域コミュニティバス「本田・天王号」|publisher=Shibata City|accessdate=2022-03-10}}. * From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsukioka Station (Niigata)|ja|月岡駅 (新潟県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take a taxi for about 10 minutes (some inns offer free shuttle service). * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the reservation-only bus "Tsukioka Onsen – Gotō Onsen-kō Shūyū Liner" (via {{ill|Shibata Castle|ja|新発田城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Cite web|url=https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/tsukioka.php|title=月岡温泉・五頭温泉郷周遊ライナー|publisher=Niigata Airport|accessdate=2021-05-30}}. * From Tokyo (Shinjuku/Ikebukuro), use the Izumi Kankō Bus "NETWORK 821" (operates on specific days such as Saturdays) and alight at "Tsukioka Onsen"[http://www.izumi-group.jp/expressbus/ 高速路線バス] - Izumi Kankō Bus. Accessed December 23, 2018.. ; By Car * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tōhoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyoe Niigata Higashi Port Interchange|ja|豊栄新潟東港IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Approximately 20 minutes from the {{ill|Ban'etsu Expressway|ja|磐越自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yasuda Interchange|ja|安田IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * [[SPA SONIC]] – A music festival held at a hot spring. * [[Tsukioka Animal Park]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Niigata Prefecture]] * [[Toyo Onsen]], [[Hanesawa Onsen]], [[Senami Onsen]] – Other hot springs associated with petroleum extraction. == External Links == * [http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/ Tsukioka Onsen Tourism Association] ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.tsukiokaonsen.gr.jp/img/common/tsukioka-map.pdf Tsukioka Onsen Town Map]}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|月岡温泉|version=104109443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Bb']] [[Category:Sulfur springs]] }} [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[ja:月岡温泉]] [[ja:川湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:箱根湯本温泉]] [[ja:日光湯元温泉]] [[ja:草津温泉]] [[ja:万座温泉]] [[ja:野沢温泉]] [[ja:野湯]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[en:Tsukioka Onsen]]
